notes: kiro-cli is the IN-PLACE (same-session) variant of checkpoint-rewind — the kiro analogue of claudecode /rewind, NOT codex /fork. CORRECTED from the live recording 2026-06-05-04-09-00 (record backflow): the changelog phrase 'continue in a new session' is a UX label, not a new <uuid>.jsonl — kiro 2.5.1 /rewind rewinds the SAME session in place (the picker reads 'Fork from a previous prompt in this session' / 'Enter to fork', but the fork stays within the same ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl, which kiro TRUNCATES back to the rewound point then continues appending). Source is FilesUnderRoot(~/.kiro/sessions/cli): a launch pre-session enters ready and hands off to a UUID-1 session on the first prompt. UUID-1 runs TWO normal turns ('one','two') — the checkpoints to rewind back from. Then /rewind opens an arrow-key picker (Down/Enter); confirming rewinds UUID-1 in place and the post-rewind turn ('three') runs on the SAME UUID-1 from the rewound point. NO second session_id is ever born — the live recording proved exactly one keyed session (6ddfde37) across the whole run; the v2_session_birth fork assertion that the doc-based assessment carried was REFUTED. Two user-observable signals: (1) the session stays ready immediately after the rewind confirm (the rewind itself is not a user turn), asserted as a post_rewind_ready dwell; (2) the post-rewind turn drives a clean ready→working→ready arc on the SAME UUID-1 (same_session_as v1_session_handoff). agent_supports=partial: /rewind reverts conversation state only (continues from an earlier prompt; no file-edit checkpoint restore — prior tool-applied edits stay in the working tree). daemon_capability=full: the daemon faithfully tracks the rewind as same-session transcript activity (working→ready) — the 3-state model represents the in-place rewind correctly; no fork linkage is needed because there is no fork. Observations: kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost, so the model assertion is omitted (kiro emits none), cost_nonzero false, tokens_nonzero false.

text: Launch pre-session row appears in ready within 2s of kiro-cli chat launch
PID bound by cwd match within 2s

After the first prompt lands, kiro writes its <uuid>.jsonl and the keyed UUID-1 session appears in ready — the pre-session hands off to a fresh UUID-1 (bound generous to cover first-token latency before kiro writes its transcript)
First user prompt ('one') drives ready → working on the UUID-1 session
First turn completes back to ready (text-only AssistantMessage) on UUID-1 — checkpoint 1
Second user prompt ('two') drives ready → working on the same UUID-1
Second turn completes back to ready on UUID-1 — checkpoint 2, the point to rewind back from

Spec: 'Session row reflects the rewound state with state ready' — after the /rewind picker confirm, the session stays ready (the rewind itself is not a user turn) and the UUID-1 file is rewound IN PLACE (no transcript_removed — same session, not a fork).

Spec: 'Subsequent turns start cleanly from the rewound point: ready → working' — the post-rewind prompt ('three') drives ready → working on the SAME UUID-1 (kiro rewinds in place; no new session_id), from the rewound point.
Spec: post-rewind turn completes back to ready on the SAME UUID-1 — a clean ready → working → ready arc from the rewound point; final live state is ready.
